title: Pedilonum longicalcaratum
intro: Pedilonum longicalcaratum, also known as the long-beaked Pedilonum or long-beaked Pedilanthus, is a plant species in the Euphorbiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, providing relief from fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pedilonum longicalcaratum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly used to treat respiratory problems and fever in traditional medicine.
taxonomy: Pedilonum longicalcaratum is a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae, which is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the madder family. The Rubiaceae family is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants, with over 13,000 species. As a member of the Rubiaceae family, Pedilonum longicalcaratum is classified in the kingdom Plantae, phylum Angiospermophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. The plant is further classified into the order Gentianales, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes the families Apocynaceae and Loganiaceae. Pedilonum longicalcaratum is also known to belong to the genus Pedilonum, which is a genus of flowering plants endemic to certain regions.
